entries.name: Carriage House
entries.description:
You have a carriage, two goats to pull it, and a stable. A second upgrade improves the carriage with armor and larger, swifter goats.

Horses are very rare in Doskvol— most carriages in the city use the large Akorosian goat as their draft animal.

entries.name: Camouflage
entries.description: Your vehicles are perfectly concealed when at rest. They blend in as part of the environment, or as an uninteresting civilian vehicle (your choice).

entries.name: Assassin Rigging
entries.description: You get 2 free load worth of weapon or gear items. For example, you could carry a pistol (a weapon) and burglary tools (gear) for zero load.

entries.name: Ironhook Contacts
entries.description: Your Tier is effectively +1 higher in prison. This counts for any Tier-related element in prison, including the incarceration roll (see page 148).

entries.name: Elite Rovers
entries.description: All of your cohorts with the Rovers type get +1d to quality rolls for Rover-related actions.

entries.name: Ordained
entries.description: Each PC gets +1 trauma box. This costs three upgrades to unlock, not just one. This may bring a PC with 4 trauma back into play if you wish.

entries.name: Training: Playbook
entries.description: If you have a Training upgrade, you earn 2 xp (instead of 1) when you train a Playbook during downtime. This upgrade essentially helps you advance more quickly. See Advancement, page 48.

entries.name: Smuggler Rigging
entries.description: Two of your carried items are perfectly concealed. You could carry 1 load of contraband and a pistol, perfectly concealed, even against a pat down.

entries.name: Vault
entries.description: Your lair has a secure vault, increasing your storage capacity for coin to 8. A second upgrade increases  your capacity to 16. A separate part of your vault can be used as a holding cell.

entries.name: Ironhook Contacts
entries.description: Your Tier is effectively +1 higher in prison. This counts for any Tier-related element in prison, including the incarceration roll (see page 148).

entries.name: Elite Adepts
entries.description: All of your cohorts with the Adepts type get +1d to quality rolls for Adept-related actions.

entries.name: Hidden Lair
entries.description: Your lair has a secret location and is disguised to hide it from view. If your lair is discovered, use two downtime activities and pay coin equal to your Tier to relocate it and hide it once again.

entries.name: Hardened
entries.description: Each PC gets +1 trauma box. This costs three upgrades to unlock, not just one. This may bring a PC with 4 trauma back into play if you wish.

entries.name: Training: Prowess
entries.description: If you have a Training upgrade, you earn 2 xp (instead of 1) when you train a Prowess during downtime. This upgrade essentially helps you advance more quickly. See Advancement, page 48.

entries.name: Workshop
entries.description: Your lair has a workshop appointed with tools for tinkering and alchemy, as well as a small library of books, documents, and maps. You may accomplish long-term projects with these assets without leaving your lair.

entries.name: Elite Thugs
entries.description: All of your cohorts with the Thugs type get +1d to quality rolls for Thug-related actions.

entries.name: Cult Rigging
entries.description: You get 2 free load worth of document or implement items. For example, you could carry a profane book of curses and a demon’s hand for zero load.

entries.name: Boat House
entries.description: You have a boat, adock on a waterway, and a small shack to store boating supplies. A second upgrade improves the boat with armor and more cargo capacity.

entries.name: Bravos Rigging
entries.description: You get 2 free load worth of weapon or armor items. For example, you could carry a sword & pistol or wear normal armor for zero load.

entries.name: Thief Rigging
entries.description: You get 2 free load worth of tool or gear items. For example, you could carry burglary gear and tinkering tools for zero load.

entries.name: Mastery
entries.description: Your crew has access to master level training. You may advance your PCs’ action ratings to 4 (until you unlock this upgrade, PC action ratings are capped at 3). This costs four upgrade boxes to unlock.

entries.name: Hawker Rigging
entries.description: One carried item is concealed and has no load. For example, you could carry a load of drugs or a weapon, perfectly concealed, for zero load.

entries.name: Ritual Sanctum in Lair
entries.description: This counts as a sacred and arcane workshop for occult practices and rituals.

entries.name: Secure Lair
entries.description:
Your lair has locks, alarms, and traps to thwart intruders. A second upgrade improves the defenses to include arcane measures that work against spirits.

 You might roll your crew’s Tier if these measures are ever put to the test, to see how well they thwart an intruder.

entries.name: Underground Maps and Passkeys
entries.description: You have easy passage through the underground canals, tunnels, and basements of the city.

entries.name: Quality
entries.description: Each upgrade improves the quality rating of all the PCs’ items of that type, beyond the quality established by the crew’s Tier and fine items. You can improve the quality of Documents, Gear (covers BurglaryGear and Climbing Gear), Arcane Implements, Subterfuge Supplies, Tools (covers Demolitions Tools and Tinkering Tools), and Weapons.

entries.name: Quarters
entries.description: Your lair includes living quarters for the crew. Without this upgrade, each PC sleeps elsewhere, and is vulnerable when they do so.

entries.name: Composed
entries.description: Each PC gets +1 stress box. This costs three upgrades to unlock, not just one.

entries.name: Training: Resolve
entries.description: If you have a Training upgrade, you earn 2 xp (instead of 1) when you train a Resolve during downtime. This upgrade essentially helps you advance more quickly. See Advancement, page 48.

entries.name: Elite Skulks
entries.description: All of your cohorts with the Skulks type get +1d to quality rolls for Skulk-related actions.

entries.name: Steady
entries.description: Each PC gets +1 stress box. This costs three upgrades to unlock, not just one.

entries.name: Elite Rooks
entries.description: All of your cohorts with the Rooks type get +1d to quality rolls for Rook-related actions.

entries.name: Ironhook Contacts
entries.description: Your Tier is effectively +1 higher in prison. This counts for any Tier-related element in prison, including the incarceration roll (see page 148).

entries.name: Training: Insight
entries.description: If you have a Training upgrade, you earn 2 xp (instead of 1) when you train a Insight during downtime. This upgrade essentially helps you advance more quickly. See Advancement, page 48.

entries.name: Barge
entries.description: Add mobility to your lair. You can move it to a new location as a downtime activity.

entries.name: Hardened
entries.description: Each PC gets +1 trauma box. This costs three upgrades to unlock, not just one. This may bring a PC with 4 trauma back into play if you wish.

entries.name: Vehicle
entries.description: All smugglers start with a vehicle. When the vehicle is upgraded (two boxes), it also gets armor.
